items.title: Overflow and containment: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? overflow:hidden is a safe universal fix for any layout that becomes too large.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Resolve the sizing cause first and use accessible scrolling where content must remain available; do not hide meaningful overflow blindly.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Resolve the sizing cause first and use accessible scrolling where content must remain available; do not hide meaningful overflow blindly.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Resolve the sizing cause first and use accessible scrolling where content must remain available; do not hide meaningful overflow blindly.
overflow:hidden is a safe universal fix for any layout that becomes too large.

items.title: Overflow and containment: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Handle long words, scroll regions and clipping without hiding essential content or focus. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating long-token wrapping without essential clipping; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; } .panel { inline-size: min(100%, 42rem); min-inline-size: 0; padding: 1rem; border: 2px solid; overflow-wrap: anywhere; aspect-ratio: auto; }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for long-token wrapping without essential clipping. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
